message:
refactor: Add optional timestamp parameter to delete_issue for sync control

Extend SqliteStorage::delete_issue() to accept an optional deleted_at
timestamp, enabling the sync system to preserve original deletion times
when importing tombstones from JSONL files.

API change:
- delete_issue(id, actor, reason) -> delete_issue(id, actor, reason, deleted_at)
- When deleted_at is None, uses Utc::now() (existing behavior)
- When deleted_at is Some(timestamp), uses the provided timestamp

This change is preparation for proper 3-way merge support where:
1. JSONL imports may contain tombstones with historical deletion times
2. Preserving original timestamps enables accurate conflict detection
3. The sync system can determine which deletion is "newer" when merging

Files updated:
- src/cli/commands/delete.rs: Pass None for interactive deletions
- tests/storage_crud.rs: Update all delete_issue test calls
- tests/storage_list_filters.rs: Update tombstone test call

All existing callers pass None to maintain current behavior. The storage
layer implementation (sqlite.rs) will be updated in a subsequent commit
as part of the larger external dependency resolution feature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
